svg.title: Double-slit fringe intensity pattern I(theta) = cos^2(pi d sin(theta)/lambda)
svg.desc: Single-panel line plot of double-slit intensity I proportional to cos^2(pi*u) versus u = d*sin(theta)/lambda from -3 to +3. The primary wavelength lambda (solid ink) shows seven peaks at integer u values. An overlaid dashed red curve for half the wavelength (double momentum) shows twice as many fringes in the same angular range, illustrating that faster electrons produce finer interference fringes.
svg.text: Double-slit intensity I(theta) = cos²(pi d sin(theta) / lambda)
